text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that can understand and generate human-like text. They work by processing vast amounts of text data, such as books, articles, and websites, learning patterns in language. Through this training, the models can predict the next word in a sentence or respond to questions. Users interact with LLMs through prompts, carefully crafted instructions that guide the output. This skill, known as prompt engineering, helps get better results. LLMs can write essays, answer questions, or even create stories. However, they have limits. They may produce incorrect information or reflect biases from their training data. They lack true understanding or common sense, relying purely on learned patterns, so it is wise to verify their responses.
